name: Challah
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Active dry yeast or 2 scant tablespoons
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir together the yeast, 1 cup of warm water, and sugar. Set aside for 10 minutes until it bubbles.
2. Beat 3 eggs with honey in a separate bowl.
3. Add the remaining 1/2 cup of water, oil, and salt to the egg mixture. Stir well.
4. Gradually add yeast mixture into flour, one cup at a time, stirring after each addition.
5. Once all flour is incorporated, mix until the dough is sticky but no longer falls apart when lifted from the bowl.
6. If using raisins, fold them in now.
7. Transfer dough to a floured surface and knead until smooth (about 8 minutes).
8. Shape dough into a ball, place in an oiled bowl, cover with a towel, and let rise for 1-2 hours or until nearly doubled in size.
9. Punch down the risen dough and divide it into thirds. Divide each third into three equal pieces.
10. Roll each piece into long ropes. Braid the ropes as you would hair, securing ends by pressing them down.
11. Place loaves on a greased baking sheet or 9-inch round pans, cover with a towel, and let rise for an additional 30 minutes or until doubled in size again.
12. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
13. Brush the loaves with remaining egg mixed with water. Sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds.
14. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
Nutritional info per slice (15/loaf): 141 cal; 3.2g pro, 25.4g carb, 3.1g fat (20%), 1g fiber, 17.9mg chol. 78mg sodium Exchanges: .3fruit, 1.1 bread, .08 meat, .5 fat
